methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $160M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
RobCo is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Roman Hölzl that has raised $160M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine same-sector peers at comparable or adjacent stages, including eight private companies (Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and others) and one public comparable, with valuations spanning $300M to $10.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $160M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $272M. Peer-median analysis across the nine comparable companies produced a median valuation of $1.6B, anchored by reference points including Hadrian at $1.6B (Series C), Dexterity at $1.6B (Series B), and Exotec at $2.0B (Series D).

These methods triangulate to a range of $231M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $936M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in stage-equivalent positioning within a sector marked by significant valuation variance. The peer median of $1.6B is materially higher than stage-progression output, suggesting either that RobCo's performance trajectory trails peer cohorts or that the $160M raised proxy understates operational maturity. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to named peers and whether RobCo's specific stage positioning tracks more closely to Series B or Series D equivalents.
